finding_results.notes: Roughened all five cited locations. (1) Jet's confession at :313 replaced 'is ours too' thesis with self-correcting procedure language ('I keep calling this her problem. It's not her problem. Half that file is the building I worked in.'). (2) Removed the narration block (:315-317) that spotlit Jet's line as 'Too clean' — the self-aware commentary was itself a form of admiring the craft. Replaced with a flat beat ('Nobody answered that.') and a shorter evasion line. (3) Jet's follow-up at :319 now continues the thought instead of walking back a thesis ('Forget that' removed since the rougher first line needs no retraction; 'somebody else's pile' replaces 'some stranger's file'). (4) Spike's route speech at :337 replaced 'Keep things moving and don't ask who's breathing because of it' (quotable aphorism) with flat procedural finish ('You'd sign it through... That was it.'). (5) Spike's admission at :345 replaced 'the boxes stopped being boxes' (compressed metaphor) with concrete factual statement ('I knew the manifests had clinic codes and I never looked at what was under them') plus trailing flat admission ('Didn't want to.'). (6) Meta-commentary at former :349-351 ('the most direct thing' / 'if you admired a line from Spike, he'd take it back') removed entirely, replaced at :347 with sensory silence beat ('The galley was quiet except for the pan cooling on the burner, metal ticking as it contracted.'). No collateral changes to surrounding dialogue or narration.

summary: Single finding addressed: the galley confessional's thesis-quality admissions from Jet and Spike were roughened into procedure language, self-correction, and concrete flat statement. Narrator meta-commentary admiring Spike's line was removed. All five cited evidence locations revised; no new continuity issues introduced.
